changes.title: DARIAH Innovation Forum #1 in Aarhus: Bringing Together DARIAH and the Industries

More than at any other point in our annual calendar, the creation of this document each year gives us a chance to reflect on our achievements as an infrastructure, and, in particular, on the question of how we have made a difference for researchers in Europe. 2019

changes.after: Date: 28 September 2020 – 11:00 to 12:30Location: Online SSHOC (Task 5.2) is developing a data repository service for SSH institutions. Dataverse is an open source software platform, community driven, and allows integrations with other data services such as DataCite or ROpenScience.

changes.after: DARIAH, the Digital Research Infrastructure for the Arts and Humanities, is a European research infrastructure which aims to enhance and support digitally-enabled research and teaching across the arts and humanities. changes.title: ECHOES Cascading Grants Programme – Second Call on Engagement and Collaboration
changes.before: What is the ECCCH? The ECCCH is a new EU-funded digital platform connecting Cultural Heritage Institutions (CHIs) across Europe, including museums, archives, libraries, researchers, and conservators. It offers shared infrastructure, advanced tools (AI, 3D, annotation), and a collaborative space to digitise, preserve, and showcase cultural heritage. Why join? Collaborate across disciplines and bordersManage, and share
changes.after: What is the ECCCH? The ECCCH is a new EU-funded digital platform connecting Cultural Heritage Institutions (CHIs) across Europe, including museums, archives, libraries, researchers, and conservators. It offers shared infrastructure, advanced tools (AI, 3D, annotation), and a collaborative space to digitise, preserve, and showcase cultural heritage. Why join?
